data.title: RamSoft partners with Koios Medical to offer AI-powered software for cancer diagnosis. Agreement announced on Sept. 25, 2025.
data.description: RamSoft Inc., a global leader in cloud-based RIS/PACS radiology solutions, has recently revealed a distributorship agreement that aims to boost detection capabilities and streamline workflows for clinicians. This collaboration will introduce an integrated solution with Koios software, promising to enhance efficiencies in healthcare settings. The news was announced in Toronto and New York on September 25, 2025. This partnership is expected to significantly impact clinicians' abilities to improve detection, while also optimizing workflow processes.

data.title: Brookfield Business Partners will convert BBU and BBUC into BBU Inc., simplifying its corporate structure.
data.description: Today, in Brookfield, a noteworthy announcement was made by Brookfield Business Partners on September 25, 2025. They have given the green light to a plan that involves streamlining their corporate setup. This will involve the conversion of Brookfield Business Partners LP (known as "BBU") and Brookfield Business Corporation (known as "BBUC") into a single publicly traded Canadian corporation, to be known as "BBU Inc." This decision marks a significant move towards simplification in their structure.

data.title: NFR awards Borregaard ASA NOK 15 million grant for Biomer project, developing bio-based alternatives to NOK 15 million fossil-based polymers.
data.description: NOK 15 million has been granted by the Norwegian Research Council (NFR) to back Borregaard's Biomer innovation initiative. This project targets the creation of bio-based substitutes for polycarboxylates, traditional fossil-based polymers extensively utilized in agriculture, industry, and household items.

data.title: BizClik honors recipients of Global Procurement & Supply Chain Awards 2025 highlighting best in resilience, digital transformation, sustainability, inclusion.
data.description: The Awards highlight companies and individuals addressing key issues in global supply chains, such as resilience, digital transformation, sustainability, and inclusion. On September 25, 2025, in London, BizClik, a leading B2B media and events organization, collaborated to acknowledge these efforts.

data.title: Drugs Made In America Acquisition II Corp. prices $500M IPO, offering 50M units at $10 each on Nasdaq (DMIIU).
data.description: Fort Lauderdale, FL on September 25, 2025 - Drugs Made In America Acquisition II Corp. (Nasdaq: DMIIU) disclosed the pricing of its recent initial public offering, unveiling 50,000,000 units at $10.00 per unit. The units are set to be listed on The Nasdaq Global Market (“Nasdaq”) and will trade under the ticker symbol “DMIIU,” effective September 25, 2025. Each unit encompasses one ordinary share and one right entitling one-tenth (1/10) of an ordinary share upon the completion of an initial business combination. Post commencement of separate trading, the ordinary shares and rights are anticipated to be listed on Nasdaq under the symbols “DMII” and “DMIIR,” respectively. Furthermore, underwriters possess a 45-day option to acquire up to an additional 7,500,000 units from the Company to cover potential over-allotments. The offering's conclusion is projected for September 26, 2025, pending standard closing formalities.

data.title: Canada Nickel reports strong nickel mineralization in drilling results - MID25-17A: 0.30% nickel over 18.7m and 330.0m, MID25-18A: 0.29% nickel over 449.0m.
data.description: In recent findings, MID25-17A drilling revealed significant nickel mineralization just below the overburden, showing 0.30% nickel over an 18.7-meter stretch and consistently at 0.30% nickel across 330.0 meters. Meanwhile, MID25-18A drilling also displayed promising results, with 0.29% nickel over an impressive 449.0 meters, including a peak of 0.36% nickel over 10.5 meters. These successful drilling efforts have led to the extension of the outlined mineral deposit.

data.title: Avient appoints David N. Schneider as President of its Specialty Engineered Materials; He replaces Christopher Pederson.
data.description: In a recent announcement on September 25, 2025, Avient Corporation (NYSE: AVNT), a pioneer in materials solutions, welcomed David N. Schneider as the new Senior Vice President and President of Specialty Engineered Materials. Schneider takes over the position from Christopher L. Pederson.
